item.title: policy(scan): review whole payload incl. .claude/ + flag cross-service credential routing (#2360)
item.description: * policy(scan): review whole payload incl .claude/ + flag credential extraction The review rubric anchored "read every relevant file" to the loaded plugin surface (skills/*/SKILL.md, hook-referenced source) and checked credential reads (~/.ssh, ~/.aws/credentials) only within hooks. Code that reads

item.title: Update sentry plugin source to getsentry/plugin-claude (#3201)
chore(ci): re-pin validate-plugins + scan-plugins + bump-plugin-shas to community 426e469f (#3132)
item.description: * chore(bump-plugin-shas): re-pin to community 426e469f (subdir-existence guard #267) Picks up anthropics/claude-plugins-community#267 — guards subdir existence before manifest synthesis, so a strict:false external whose source.path subdir vanished at the new SHA is a clean "subdir not found" skip

item.title: ci: pin validate-plugins + scan-plugins + bump-plugin-shas to self-healing CLI install (#3095)
item.description: * ci: bump validate-plugins action pin to self-healing CLI install Bumps the pinned validate-plugins action SHA (f846a0bc -> 5d75f99) to pick up two fixes already merged in claude-plugins-community: 1. Self-healing claude CLI install (community#233): the prior pin's install step was a bare `npm

item.title: Merge pull request #3094 from anthropics/dickson/add-project-artifact-plugin
item.description: Add project-artifact plugin

item.title: Trim project-artifact README to usage essentials
Add project-artifact plugin: living project status pages published as artifacts
Merge pull request #2536 from anthropics/codemod-dynamic-workflows
item.description: code-modernization: dynamic workflow orchestration + untrusted-content hardening

item.title: Merge pull request #2696 from anthropics/venv-target-fallback
item.description: security-guidance: pip --target fallback when venv can't bootstrap pip (Option A, #2154) — 2.0.4 → 2.0.5

item.title: security-guidance: pip --target fallback when venv can't bootstrap pip (2.0.4 → 2.0.5)
item.description: Option A, the data-gated fix for venv_ensurepip_fail (#2154 follow-up). v2.0.4 telemetry made the call: of the venv_ensurepip_fail cohort, ~95% HAVE pip (sdk_has_pip=true) and run Python 3.11–3.14 — so it's not the Apple-3.9 problem; it's modern interpreters where `python -m venv` can't bootstrap p

item.title: Merge pull request #2311 from anthropics/readd-valtown
item.description: Re-add valtown plugin entry

item.title: Merge pull request #2306 from anthropics/add-vals
item.description: Add vals plugin

item.title: Update aws-startup-advisor listing description (#2305)
item.description: Refreshes the marketplace listing description for the AWS Startup Advisor plugin at the maintainer's request. Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

item.title: Merge pull request #2193 from anthropics/fix-2159-stop-hook-schema
item.description: security-guidance: emit schema-valid Stop-hook output (#2159) — 2.0.2 → 2.0.3

item.title: security-guidance: emit schema-valid Stop-hook output (#2159) — 2.0.2 → 2.0.3
item.description: Fixes #2159. The Stop hook emits feedback via `hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext}`, but `Stop`/`SubagentStop` are NOT members of CC's `hookSpecificOutput` discriminated union (coreSchemas.ts — valid members are PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, etc.

item.title: bump: switch to per-entry PR mode (one PR per stale plugin) (#2051)
item.description: * bump: switch to per-entry PR mode (one PR per stale plugin) Replaces the single batched bump PR with one PR per stale plugin so a single failing plugin no longer blocks the rest. Pins to a feature branch of the bump-plugin-shas action that adds 'pr-mode: per-entry'; re-pin to the merge commit on

item.title: Merge pull request #2105 from anthropics/fix-2098-output-config-format
item.description: security-guidance: migrate _call_claude from deprecated output_format to output_config.format (#2098)

item.title: security-guidance: migrate from deprecated output_format to output_config.format (#2098)
item.description: Fixes #2098. The Anthropic Messages API moved structured-output schema specification from a top-level `output_format` field to a nested `output_config.format` field, per https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/structured-outputs. Per docs the old form "will continue working for a tran

item.title: Merge pull request #2101 from anthropics/fix-2099-quotepath-global
item.description: security-guidance: move core.quotePath=false to GIT_CMD globally (#2099 followup)

item.title: security-guidance: move core.quotePath=false to GIT_CMD globally (#2099 followup)
item.description: Followup to PR #2086 (which added the flag to 4 specific git call sites) and PR #2100 (text=True purge for #2099). The Windows reporter for #2099 noticed more git invocations still lacked the flag — rev-parse path queries (--show-toplevel, --git-dir, --git-common-dir), reflog %gs subjects, and `git

item.title: Merge pull request #2100 from anthropics/fix-2099-text-true-pythonutf8
item.description: security-guidance: purge text=True from subprocess.run + bake PYTHONUTF8=1 (#2099)

item.title: security-guidance: purge text=True from subprocess.run + bake PYTHONUTF8=1 (#2099)
item.description: URGENT WINDOWS FIX. Sibling of #2056 / PR #2075 but covering 14 more sites that PR #2075 missed. The bug class: on Windows with cp1252 default encoding (typical en-US locale), `subprocess.run(..., text=True)` decodes child stdout AND stderr via `locale.getpreferredencoding()`. When git emits a UTF-

item.title: Merge pull request #2078 from anthropics/fix-1868-claude-config-dir
item.description: security-guidance: respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for plugin state files (#1868)

item.title: Merge pull request #2091 from anthropics/fix-2089-if-clause-regression
item.description: URGENT: security-guidance: fix #2089 regression — split |-joined if clauses

item.title: security-guidance: 5 precise if entries fixing #2089 regression + gt support
item.description: URGENT REGRESSION FIX. PR #2076 (Graphite gt workflow) gated the PostToolUse commit/push hooks with: "if": "Bash(git commit:*)|Bash(gt create:*)|Bash(gt modify:*)" "if": "Bash(git push:*)|Bash(gt submit:*)" mirroring the regex-OR idiom that `matcher` uses ("Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdi

item.title: Merge pull request #2086 from anthropics/fix-2082-diff-parser-non-ascii
item.description: security-guidance: pass core.quotePath=false to diff feeders (#2082)

item.title: security-guidance: pass core.quotePath=false to diff feeders (#2082)
item.description: Fixes anthropics/claude-plugins-official#2082 — diff feeders use git's default quotePath setting, which C-quotes any path with a non-ASCII byte. The downstream parsers in gitutil.parse_diff_into_files / gitutil.extract_file_paths_from_diff match the diff header with `re.match(r'^a/(.+?) b/(.+)$', ..

item.title: Merge pull request #2077 from anthropics/fix-1358-1375-1783-hook-output-protocol
item.description: security-guidance: emit findings via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext (#1358 #1375 #1783)

item.title: security-guidance: respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for plugin state files (#1868)
item.description: Fixes #1868 — when CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is set to a non-default location (e.g. ~/.config/claude for XDG compliance, or a multi-tenant install path), the plugin still wrote state files to the hardcoded ~/.claude/ path, leaving stale state and breaking CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR's purpose. Resolution precedence

item.title: security-guidance: emit findings via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext (#1358 #1375 #1783)
item.description: Fixes #1358, #1375, and #1783 — three related complaints about the hook output protocol used at the three asyncRewake exit-2 sites (handle_commit_review_posttooluse, handle_push_sweep_posttooluse, handle_stop_hook). The old shape at each site was: emit_metrics({...})

item.title: Merge pull request #2076 from anthropics/fix-2048-graphite-gt-workflow
item.description: security-guidance: detect Graphite (gt) commands as commit/push events (#2048)

item.title: Merge pull request #2075 from anthropics/fix-2056-windows-unicode-decode
item.description: security-guidance: lenient UTF-8 decode in 6 git-subprocess helpers (#2056)

item.title: security-guidance: detect Graphite (gt) commands as commit/push events (#2048)
item.description: Fixes anthropics/claude-plugins-official#2048 — teams using Graphite for stacked PRs (`gt create` / `gt modify` / `gt submit`) never get the commit/push agentic review because the hook matcher only catches literal `git commit` / `git push` Bash calls. gt shells out to git as a subprocess, but the ho

item.title: Merge pull request #2074 from anthropics/fix-xss-rules-non-js-false-positives
item.description: security-guidance: gate XSS pattern rules to JS-family files

item.title: security-guidance: lenient UTF-8 decode in 6 git-subprocess helpers (#2056)
item.description: Fixes anthropics/claude-plugins-official#2056 — on Windows, when the worktree contains an untracked file whose name has a character undefined in cp1252 (accented capitals like Á Í Ï Ð Ý, most CJK, emoji), the UserPromptSubmit hook crashes: Exception in thread Thread-5 (_readerthread): Unicode

item.title: security-guidance: gate XSS pattern rules to JS-family files
item.description: Closes #410, #2037, #2045, #1640, #1280, #1329, #1341, #255, anthropics/claude-code#46720 (partial closes on overlap with other rules). The plugin's substring-only XSS / browser-DOM rules (new_function_injection, react_dangerously_set_html, document_write_xss, innerHTML_xss, outerHTML_xss, insertAd

item.title: Merge pull request #2073 from anthropics/fix-2071-macos-python-39
item.description: security-guidance: enable LLM review on default macOS Python 3.9 (#2071)

item.title: security-guidance: enable LLM review on default macOS Python 3.9
item.description: Fixes anthropics/claude-plugins-official#2071 — on macOS where the default `python3` is Apple's Command Line Tools Python 3.9.6, the plugin's agentic commit reviewer silently does not run, even when the user has a newer Python installed. Three compounding factors in the bug: 1. `sg-python.sh` only
